title: Razor Smoky Indian Long Bean Jalebi
description: A smoky, shattering sweet jalebi infused with charred long beans for a comforting sick‑day treat.

ingredients.name: Sugar syrup
ingredients.amount: 1 cup sugar, 1/2 cup water, 1 tsp lemon juice, pinch nutmeg

instructions: Wash and trim long beans, cut into 1/2 inch pieces.
In a saucepan, heat 1 tbsp butter, add bean pieces, and sauté until slightly charred to develop smoky flavor.
Remove beans and blend into a fine puree with a pinch of salt.
In a mixing bowl, whisk 1 cup flour, 1 tbsp sugar, 1/2 tsp cardamom, 1/4 tsp baking soda and 1 tsp ginger powder.
Add 1 cup yogurt, 1/4 cup warm water, and the bean puree to the dry mix, stir to a smooth batter.
Cover and let the batter ferment for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Heat 1 tsp coconut oil in a deep pan to 180°C; perform a water test to confirm temperature.
Using a piping bag fitted with a star tip, pipe small coils of batter into the hot oil, frying until golden and crisp.
While jalebis are frying, prepare syrup by boiling 1 cup sugar, 1/2 cup water, 1 tsp lemon juice, and a pinch nutmeg until it reaches a soft‑ball stage.
Once jalebis are done, dip them into the hot syrup for 10 seconds, then set aside to soak for 1 minute.
Serve warm; the smoky long bean flavor infuses the sweet jalebi, ideal for a sick‑day comfort snack.
